title: Puffy Bitter Slovakian Buckwheat Pancakes
description: A traditional Slovakian dish featuring dark brown soft sugar and buckwheat, these pancakes are bitter, fluffy, and nostalgic, made with a special ice-bath-shock step and cooked in a copper mug to create a unique and delicious treat, perfect for those looking for a FODMAP-friendly option.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the buckwheat flour, dark brown soft sugar, and baking powder.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and vanilla extract.
Add the melted butter to the egg mixture and whisk until combined.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and grease with a small amount of oil.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter onto the skillet or griddle.
Cook the pancakes for 2-3 minutes, until bubbles appear on the surface and the edges start to dry.
Using a spatula, carefully place the cooked pancakes into a copper mug filled with ice water to stop the cooking process and create the puffy texture.
Let the pancakes sit in the ice water for 30 seconds to 1 minute, then remove and serve immediately.
